channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Indian activist Wangchuk ends 26-day hunger strike as 'Cockroach' protesters vow to press on
item.description: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike on Friday that helped galvanize a youth movement that's become one of India's largest protests in recent years, but demonstrators vowed to continue until the education minister resigns.
item.content:encoded:
Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike on Friday that helped galvanize a youth movement that's become one of India's largest protests in recent years, but demonstrators vowed to continue until the education minister resigns.

(Image credit: Vipin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rubio says U.S. is ready to help end war in Ukraine but no quick deal ahead
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who met with his Russian counterpart Sergey Lavrov, said that President Trump was committed to the work "if conditions and factors have changed to make that possible."
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who met with his Russian counterpart Sergey Lavrov, said that President Trump was committed to the work "if conditions and factors have changed to make that possible."

(Image credit: BRENDAN SMIALOWSKI)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In a first, Chinese woman wins the prestigious Fields Medal
item.description: Despite criticisms of a brain drain from China to the U.S., the awards come at a time when several high-profile Chinese scholars have returned home, some citing an increasingly hostile academic environment in America.
item.content:encoded: Despite criticisms of a brain drain from China to the U.S., the awards come at a time when several high-profile Chinese scholars have returned home, some citing an increasingly hostile academic environment in America.
item.dc:creator: Jan Camenzind Broomby

item.title: U.S. signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia. And, Nolan Wells' independent autopsy results
item.description: The U.S. has signed a nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia. And, the independent autopsy results of Nolan Wells fuel more questions surrounding his cause of death.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. has signed a nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia. And, the independent autopsy results of Nolan Wells fuel more questions surrounding his cause of death.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia's nuclear deal hinges on the kingdom normalizing Israel ties
item.description: After a Saudi nuclear power deal raised alarm in Israel, President Trump announced online that the arrangement is subject to the kingdom normalizing relations with Israel through the Abraham Accords.
item.content:encoded:
After a Saudi nuclear power deal raised alarm in Israel, President Trump announced online that the arrangement is subject to the kingdom normalizing relations with Israel through the Abraham Accords.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: U.S. signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
item.description: The agreement between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.
item.content:encoded: The agreement between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Former State Department official on the implications of the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Daniel Benaim, a former State Department official who worked on Gulf issues, about the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Daniel Benaim, a former State Department official who worked on Gulf issues, about the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Ebola death toll in DR Congo surpasses 1,000
item.description: Congo's Ebola outbreak has crossed the grim 1,000-death threshold, marking the fastest fatal surge in recorded history of the virus.
item.content:encoded:
Congo's Ebola outbreak has crossed the grim 1,000-death threshold, marking the fastest fatal surge in recorded history of the virus.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: Beloved educator's detention fuels India's biggest protests in years
item.description: In India, the detention of a beloved educator on hunger strike has turned into its largest anti-government protests in years.
item.content:encoded: In India, the detention of a beloved educator on hunger strike has turned into its largest anti-government protests in years.
item.dc:creator: Omkar Khandekar

item.title: Trump administration signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
item.description: The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.
item.content:encoded:
The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond
